abstract: The object for displaying components side by side in a Container component.
overview:
## Discussion

Use the `HorizontalStackDisplay` object in a container component for displaying components side by side.

Use `HorizontalStackDisplay` if you wish to specify what percent of the width the individual children should use. Use [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/CollectionDisplay] if you wish to make all child components to have the same width.

To redistribute the empty space inside a horizontal stack collection, use the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/FlexibleSpacer] object.

This object can be used in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Header], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Container], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Section], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Chapter], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Aside], and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleLink].

source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/HorizontalStackDisplay
crawled: 2026-03-10T14:19:35Z
---

# HorizontalStackDisplay

**Object**

The object for displaying components side by side in a Container component.

## Declaration

```swift
object HorizontalStackDisplay
```

## Discussion

Use the `HorizontalStackDisplay` object in a container component for displaying components side by side.

Use `HorizontalStackDisplay` if you wish to specify what percent of the width the individual children should use. Use [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/CollectionDisplay] if you wish to make all child components to have the same width.

To redistribute the empty space inside a horizontal stack collection, use the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/FlexibleSpacer] object.



This object can be used in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Header], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Container], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Section], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Chapter], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Aside], and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleLink].

### Example

```json
{
  "components": [
    {
      "role": "container",
      "contentDisplay": {
        "type": "horizontal_stack"
      },
      "components": [
        {
          "role": "title",
          "text": "Sample article",
          "layout": {
            "minimumWidth": "60cw",
            "maximumWidth": "60cw"
          }
        },
        {
          "role": "spacer"
        },
        {
          "role": "image",
          "URL": "bundle://image-1.jpg",
          "layout": {
            "minimumWidth": "35cw",
            "maximumWidth": "35cw"
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Article Structure

- **Nesting Components in an Article**: Use container components to create the component hierarchies you need for special article designs.
- **Adding a Scene to a Chapter or a Section Header**: Add a scene to your article to create special effects.
- **Creating an Article Link**: Link to an article by using the article-linking container component.
- **Displaying Components Side By Side**: Configure a side-by-side, horizontal arrangement of components for your article.
- **Header**: The component for defining the top area of an article, chapter, or section.
- **Container**: Properties shared by all container types.
- **Section**: The component for organizing an article into sections.
- **Chapter**: The component for organizing an article into chapters.
- **Aside**: The component for setting apart content that is not directly related to the article, such as promotional content.
- **CollectionDisplay**: An object used in any container component type to define how the collection of child components is presented.
- **FlexibleSpacer**: The component for redistributing empty space inside a horizontal stack collection.
- **Divider**: The component for defining a horizontal line to visually divide parts of your article.
- **ArticleLink**: The container component for creating a link to an article.
- **SupportedArticleIdentifier**: The patterns supported for article identifiers in UUID format.
- **PublisherArticleIdentifier**: The identifier provided by the publisher.
